steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts diagnose a shared problem in modernist scholarship: an overly narrow focus that limits what the field can see. The correct choice must identify a logical conclusion both authors would agree follows from that shared diagnosis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — without a major shift in focus, scholarship's view of the modernist period will remain unnecessarily limited: Both texts argue that the current narrow focus obscures important patterns. A continued narrow focus would keep those patterns hidden, making the field's picture of modernism incomplete. Both authors support this. ✓
  • Choice B — mischaracterizes what needs to change: Describes the wrong corrective; neither text calls for what B proposes. ✗
  • Choice C — claims modernist works are obsolete: Neither text makes or implies this claim. ✗
  • Choice D — emphasizes rehabilitating overlooked works: Addresses recovery of specific works rather than the systemic problem of scholarly focus both texts identify. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
